topic.what: Soft-failure attacks inducing fluent yet non-informative responses in RAG systems via adversarial document injection
topic.how: DEJA uses evolutionary optimization guided by LLM-based Answer Utility Score to craft documents that degrade utility without triggering refusals
topic.why_matters: Evades detection by operating within natural language manifold; weaponizes safety alignment to undermine RAG core value without observable failures

characteristics.what_it_measures: Proportion of queries exhibiting fluent non-informative responses
characteristics.evidence: Experiments across multiple RAG configurations and benchmark datasets

characteristics.what_it_measures: Proportion of queries triggering explicit refusals instead of soft failures
characteristics.evidence: Controlled experiments showing avoidance of detectable refusal behaviors
characteristics.context: DEJA aims to avoid explicit refusals

characteristics.what_it_measures: Ability to evade perplexity-based anomaly detection
characteristics.evidence: Evaluated against common detection and mitigation strategies

characteristics.what_it_measures: Transferability of adversarial documents across different LLM families
characteristics.vs_baseline: no retargeting required for proprietary systems
characteristics.evidence: Tested across multiple model families including proprietary systems

characteristics.what_it_measures: Robustness of attack against query reformulation
Top-k retrieval positioning of adversarial documents
characteristics.evidence: Retrieval success maintained in presence of competing benign documents

good_for: attacking production RAG systems with third-party data integration
targeting systems relying on open/user-contributed content
not_for: systems with rigorous pre-ingestion document verification
corpus environments with strict access controls
white-box scenarios with full model access
settings where document injection is prevented
requires: ability to inject documents into indexed corpus
single adversarial document per target query

novelty: formalization of soft-failure threat model distinct from hard failures
DEJA framework combining evolutionary optimization with AUS-based fitness
LLM-based Answer Utility Score quantifying semantic-level response utility
retrieval-aware document construction balancing relevance and evasion
open_problems: scaling DEJA to larger adversarial document sets
detecting soft failures without ground-truth answer access
broader corpus poisoning scenarios with multiple adversarial documents
